Gimmicks. What do you think when I say gimmicks? Because I'm going to suggest to you that everything in life is a gimmick. My jacket, okay? My tie is a gimmick. The shoes I'm wearing, it's a gimmick. So I want you to start thinking about how to use gimmicks to follow people up. Gimmicks, that's right. Everything people do every day. TV, iPods, their phones, listen to the Spotify. These are gimmicks to get money from people. When you're following up you need to think creatively with gimmicks. Flowers, cookies, ice cream, toys, baseball bats. Look, I want to hit it out of the park with you and send them a bat. You're trying to help somebody's business grow. I want to crush it with you and you send them an ice crusher. I come up with something creative, some creative way to get back in front of the customer using gimmicks. Maybe an invent invite. Hey, we're having a special VIP client invite. I want to make sure you're on our list. Even though the customers never bought from you, never done anything more than hit your website, all of a sudden you said, I put you on a VIP client invite list for a private party we're having. Lunch offers. I want to buy you lunch. I want to come into your neighborhood and buy you lunch. Donations to charities. Hey, you won a special offer for hitting our website and we want to donate money to your personal charity. That'll get them to call you back if they're at all charitable.
